name: fusiontrack_final_val_experiments
purpose: Pre-declared validation configuration for FusionTrack anomaly-detection experiments.

evaluation.main_table_rule: Use unique sample_id keys with exact label-score key matching for individual rows and unique sample_id+window_id keys with exact label-score key matching for group rows. Duplicate, missing, or extra score keys fail main-protocol runs. The group sample_id-only any-window metric is appendix-only.

fairness_rules: Use the same generated protocol files for all methods in a task.
Do not tune hyperparameters on the test split.
Do not pick per-method hyperparameters after seeing validation scores unless the run is explicitly labeled tuning or ablation.
Paper baseline rows must use official or paper-linked source code and record URL, commit, license, adapter, environment, and run manifest.
Local proxy implementations are internal/proxy/ablation rows and must not be named as paper baselines in the main table.

Deep methods start from method-appropriate initial budgets instead of forcing every method to the same epoch count; every run must record epochs, batch size, learning rate, window size, seed, adapter notes, and convergence diagnostics.
A deep-model result is final only after its train/validation loss curve satisfies the convergence policy below or after an explicitly reported non-convergence budget cap.
Individual main-protocol evaluation must have one label row and one score row per sample_id; duplicate keys, missing score keys, and extra score keys fail the run.
Group main-protocol evaluation must have one label row and one score row per sample_id+window_id; duplicate keys, missing score keys, and extra score keys fail the run.

convergence_policy.principle: Epoch counts are initial budgets, not proof of convergence.
convergence_policy.validation_split: Use only train-derived validation data or the method's official validation protocol. Do not use the anomaly evaluation labels to choose epochs.

early_stopping.monitor: validation_loss when available, otherwise training_loss plus score-stability diagnostics

convergence_policy.reporting_rule: Main tables may report a deep baseline only with convergence status: converged, early-stopped, or max-budget-not-converged.

tranad.adapter_note: Uses the official TranAD default-style n_window=10. If we need a fully standardized TSAD window comparison, run an appendix variant with individual=64 and group=16 before reporting it.

rerun_requirements: Regenerate group labels with sample_id+window_id strict protocol.
Rerun all group local baselines under group_main_key_fields.
Rerun official group baselines or reconvert their score files so group scores carry top-level window_id.
Keep old sample_id-only group results only as appendix any-window diagnostics.

Regenerate individual and group matrix configs with require_unique_keys=true and require_score_key_match=true, then rerun any experiment whose metrics show duplicate keys, missing score keys, or extra score keys.
